reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASS - function is actively used as RPC handler (registered at beacon-chain/sync/rpc.go:54). Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASS - attack path uses P2P (untrusted source). Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASS - Phase 03's code reading is factually correct: RPC by_root handler (rpc_data_column_sidecars_by_root.go:123) retrieves from storage without re-validation, and storage mixes gossip-validated columns (12 checks per beacon-chain/verification/data_column.go:26-39) with RPC by_root columns (only 3 checks: ValidFields, InclusionProven, KzgProofVerified per data_column.go:53-57). Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASS - attacker can trigger via P2P RPC by_root request providing data column with valid KZG/inclusion proofs but invalid proposer signature or finality checks; node saves to storage; later serves it to peers violating invariant. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:606-621 requires 'forall sidecar in resp: gossip_valid(sidecar.block) == true'. Code deviates from this requirement. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASS - P2P network validation is in-scope for Fusaka audit. An attacker can trigger this via P2P RPC by_root request by sending a data column with valid KZG and inclusion proofs but invalid proposer signature, causing the node to save and later re-serve invalid data to peers, spreading potentially invalid sidecars across the network. Severity justified: affects Prysm only (~31% CL share per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json client_diversity), single RPC packet trigger, network propagation risk qualifies as Medium per severity_classification (>5% network impact threshold).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Sidecars must not be served for blocks failing gossip validation in by_root responses. Assertion: forall sidecar in resp: gossip_valid(sidecar.block) == true. From outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-025 covering SG-009.
